mechanism_narrative: A persistent low level of the federal funds rate reduces the cost of capital, which continuously supports equity valuations through lower discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of non-overlapping periods over a 2-year window despite low FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations and forces downward earnings revisions, generating negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp month-over-month during a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and payroll employment indicates a self-reinforcing recovery in aggregate demand and labor income, driving positive 60-day forward returns through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and PAYEMS fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During the zero lower bound regime, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal successful monetary transmission to real activity and generate positive 20-day forward returns; this mechanism is inactive outside the zero lower bound regime.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 index points signals contracting manufacturing output and margin compression, leading to negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 0.5 points are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of occurrences during a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts exceeding 50 thousand units signals accelerating residential investment and durable goods demand, driving positive 60-day forward returns via construction and consumer spending multipliers.
failure_condition: HOUST increases exceeding 50 thousand units are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer prices alongside decreases in the unemployment rate generate a profits wedge where pricing power exceeds wage costs, lifting corporate margins and producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase and UNRATE decrease fails to predict positive 20-day returns with Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points reduces short-term funding costs for leveraged market participants, generating positive 5-day forward returns as risk positions are rapidly repriced.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS cuts exceeding 0.25pp in a month are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of events over a 2-year window.
